fault_hypothesis: The expiry check on line 61 uses strict greater-than (`now > effective_expiry`) instead of greater-than-or-equal (`now >= effective_expiry`), so entries are incorrectly served when `now` exactly equals the expiry time.

constraints.intent: At the boundary now==effective_expiry the condition should trigger a return None, meaning now>=effective_expiry should evaluate True and take the branch; the strict > misses the equality case.
Verify that effective_expiry is correctly computed so the subsequent comparison is against the right value.
After falling through the expiry branch (not returning None), now must be strictly less than effective_expiry; equality means the entry is expired and payload should not be returned.
If a payload is returned, now must be strictly less than effective_expiry; returning payload when now==expiry is the observed bug.
